cases.passage: Rahul, a 25-year-old male, was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes. His doctor prescribed him a low-calorie diet and regular exercise to manage his blood sugar levels. However, despite his efforts, Rahul's blood sugar levels remained high. His doctor then suggested adding a medication like metformin to his treatment plan. Metformin works by reducing glucose production in the liver and increasing insulin sensitivity. Rahul's doctor also advised him to get regular check-ups to monitor his blood sugar levels and make any necessary adjustments to his treatment plan. After a few months, Rahul's blood sugar levels started to come under control, and he was able to manage his condition effectively.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of treating type 2 diabetes?
opts: (A) Reducing glucose production in the liver
questions.explanation: The primary goal of treating type 2 diabetes is to manage blood sugar levels.

questions.q: What is the mechanism of action of metformin in managing type 2 diabetes?
opts: (A) Increasing glucose production in the liver
(B) Reducing glucose production in the liver
questions.explanation: Metformin works by reducing glucose production in the liver.

questions.q: Why is regular check-up important for Rahul?
opts: (A) To monitor his blood sugar levels
questions.explanation: Regular check-up is important to monitor his blood sugar levels and make any necessary adjustments to his treatment plan.

questions.q: What is the effect of metformin on insulin sensitivity?
opts: (D) Has no effect on insulin sensitivity

questions.q: Why did Rahul's doctor prescribe metformin?
opts: (A) To reduce glucose production in the liver
(C) To manage Rahul's blood sugar levels effectively
questions.explanation: Rahul's doctor prescribed metformin to manage his blood sugar levels effectively.

cases.passage: Leptospirosis is a bacterial infection caused by Leptospira bacteria. It is usually transmitted through contact with contaminated water or soil. The symptoms of leptospirosis include fever, headache, and muscle aches. In severe cases, it can lead to kidney and liver failure. A 30-year-old woman, who loves to swim in rivers, was diagnosed with leptospirosis. Her doctor prescribed her antibiotics to treat the infection. She was also advised to avoid contact with contaminated water and soil for a few weeks. After a few days of treatment, the woman started to feel better, and her symptoms subsided.

opts: (B) Through contact with contaminated water or soil
(D) Through direct contact with an infected person
questions.explanation: Leptospirosis is usually transmitted through contact with contaminated water or soil.

questions.q: What are the symptoms of leptospirosis?
opts: (C) Cough, sore throat, and runny nose
(D) Fatigue, weakness, and loss of appetite
questions.explanation: The symptoms of leptospirosis include fever, headache, and muscle aches.

questions.q: Why was the woman advised to avoid contact with contaminated water and soil?
opts: (A) To prevent the spread of the infection to others
(C) To avoid contact with contaminated water and soil for a few weeks
questions.explanation: She was advised to avoid contact with contaminated water and soil for a few weeks to prevent re-infection.

questions.q: What was the treatment prescribed for the woman?
opts: (B) Antibiotics and avoiding contact with contaminated water and soil
(C) Vaccination and avoiding contact with contaminated water and soil
(D) Rest and avoiding contact with contaminated water and soil
questions.explanation: The woman was prescribed antibiotics and advised to avoid contact with contaminated water and soil.

questions.q: What was the effect of the treatment on the woman's symptoms?
questions.explanation: The woman's symptoms subsided after a few days of treatment.
